notes: Amazon flooded with griddle scraper accessories for Blackstone/Camp Chef. Generic Chinese kits with scrapers in every price range. Saturated category, no brand gap.
Cooler dividers, baskets, and organizers widely available for Yeti/RTIC on Amazon. Multiple brands (Beast Cooler Accessories, Omoojee, etc.). No gap.

product_name: Milo Tin Scoop / Protein Powder Scoop Holder
notes: ScoopJack already exists as a branded protein scoop holder. Exactly this concept, well-executed. Market taken.

product_name: Tim Tam Slam Mug / Cookie Dunk Mug
notes: Oreo already has a branded cookie dunk mug sold at Five Below and other retailers. Novelty category is covered.
Electric heating plate requires UL/safety certifications, higher COGS, complex manufacturing. Not simple enough for solo launch.
Just a square baking pan. US has brownies pans everywhere (Wilton, USA Pan, etc.). No meaningful twist.
Vegemite isn't popular in US. Measured spreader for portion control is interesting but needs broader positioning (not Vegemite-specific).
Just long BBQ tongs. US has tons of premium grilling tongs (OXO, Weber, etc.). No real gap.
Chiko rolls aren't known in US. Egg roll formers exist but demand is weak (most people buy frozen).
Just a sandwich cutter. US has tons of kid sandwich cutters (dinosaurs, hearts, etc.) at every price point.
Campfire cooking sticks exist. Interesting concept but saturated in outdoor/camping category.
Cookie scoops/portioners are everywhere (OXO, Wilton, etc.). No meaningful gap.

product_name: Golden Gaytime Holder / Ice Cream Bar Sleeve
notes: Ice cream bar holders/sleeves exist as generic novelties. Weak demand signal, low margin potential.
Australian savory scrolls aren't a known format in US. Could work for cinnamon rolls but that's saturated.
Sand anchors for umbrellas widely available (Copa, etc.). Cooler-specific anchor is niche within niche.
Bottle openers everywhere. Flip-top/swing-top openers exist. No gap.
Just a hot dog holder. Low margin, disposable, weak brand potential.
Starbucks and generic brands already sell iced coffee shakers. Australian style (ice cream + coffee) is different but niche.
Tiny measuring spoons exist. Interesting concept for potent ingredients (fish sauce, etc.) but weak demand signal.
